title: LogiTruck Factory Archetype Registry
description: Formal registry connecting each feature archetype to its required and optional building blocks. Used by the factory to resolve context bundles automatically.

utility-feature.description: Pure TypeScript utilities, formatters, validators, converters, small helpers. Zero Firebase, zero React, deterministic input/output.

escalateToL2: utility affects payment calculations
utility affects auth or security behavior
utility requires new npm dependency
escalateToL3: utility affects pricing, commissions, fees, or payouts
utility affects investor returns or financial claims
utility involves compliance or regulatory logic

utility-feature.notes: Ideal entry point for factory validation. Pure, isolated, deterministic. No Firebase, no React, no network calls.

mobile-screen-feature.description: React Native screens across Carrier, Driver, and Finder roles. Hook-driven architecture with loading/empty/error states and navigation params.

escalateToL2: screen modifies navigation root
screen requires deep linking changes
screen touches auth or session state
screen requires map rendering
screen requires native module access

mobile-screen-feature.notes: Screen files must never contain Firestore calls directly. All data comes through hooks. Navigation changes to existing tab/drawer roots require Claude Code review.

firestore-feature.description: Firestore hooks, realtime listeners, collection queries, document sync, role-based data access patterns.

escalateToL2: hook requires async lookup before subscribe (use async-lookup-then-subscribe)
hook touches multiple collections in a single effect
hook requires security rules validation
hook requires Firestore index creation
hook involves AI session persistence
escalateToL4: factory cannot deploy Firestore security rules
factory cannot execute production data migrations

firestore-feature.notes: All async-lookup-before-subscribe patterns MUST use the ref+cancelled pattern from async-lookup-then-subscribe.md. Never use .then() chains that discard the unsubscribe return.

cloud-function-feature.description: Firebase Cloud Functions v2: callable functions, HTTP endpoints, Firestore triggers, scheduled jobs, background orchestration.

escalateToL1: function is a simple onCall with auth + Firestore write
escalateToL2: function creates a Firestore trigger
function uses Cloud Tasks fan-out
function sends push notifications
function integrates with external APIs (not OpenAI/ElevenLabs)
function touches auth or session state
function requires Firebase emulator validation
escalateToL4: factory cannot deploy functions to production
factory cannot configure Firebase secrets
factory cannot modify production indexes

cloud-function-feature.notes: Every onCall function MUST have auth check as the first statement using request.auth?.uid. Never use data.auth (v1 pattern). All errors must use HttpsError not plain Error. Export must be added to functions/index.js.

maps-tracking-feature.description: Realtime driver tracking, route rendering, ETA calculations, polyline parsing, location sync, trip progression state machine.

escalateToL1: feature involves pure JS: ETA formatting, polyline parsing, distance calc
escalateToL2: feature requires location hooks or tracking reducers
feature requires Firestore location sync
feature requires map screen rendering
escalateToL3: feature requires native map SDK installation
escalateToL4: factory cannot install native map libraries
factory cannot modify app.json or Expo plugins
factory cannot run expo prebuild

maps-tracking-feature.notes: Factory scope is limited to pure JS tracking logic. Any native map SDK, background location, or Expo plugin changes require Claude Code escalation. HomeTrackingScreen and Driver navigation root are protected.

native-integration-feature.description: Native SDK installation planning, Expo plugin impact analysis, iOS/Android config planning, background services, push native setup, voice/audio native integrations.

escalateToL1: factory generates TypeScript service drafts and mock adapters
escalateToL2: ALL native-integration-feature execution requires Claude Code
factory output is a handoff package only
escalateToL3: native permission changes require human approval (location, microphone, camera)
escalateToL4: factory cannot install native dependencies in production repo
factory cannot run pod install
factory cannot modify Gradle or Xcode project settings
factory cannot run expo prebuild against production

native-integration-feature.notes: This archetype produces a handoff package only. The factory generates plans, risk checklists, TypeScript service drafts, and mock layers. Claude Code owns all native integration, build validation, and production commit.

payments-stripe-connect-feature.description: Stripe Connect onboarding, carrier payouts, payment intents, webhook processing, transaction tracking, escrow-like operational payment flows.

escalateToL1: factory generates UI payment state screens
factory generates payload builders and reducers
escalateToL2: ALL Stripe function integration requires Claude Code review
webhook validation requires Claude Code
environment and secret configuration requires Claude Code
escalateToL3: production payout logic requires legal/human approval
transaction fee models require human approval
escalateToL4: factory cannot use production Stripe keys
factory cannot execute live payouts
factory cannot deploy production payment flows

payments-stripe-connect-feature.notes: CRITICAL: stripeconnect.js currently has a v1 onCall auth vulnerability (data.auth instead of request.auth). Factory always uses request.auth?.uid. Idempotency guards required on all Stripe account creation. Webhook handler for account.updated is always required alongside createStripeAccount.

ai-support-agent-feature.description: AI-powered support and operational assistant features: chat UI, session orchestration, prompt templates, Firebase session persistence, escalation flows.

escalateToL1: factory generates chat UI and session state reducers
factory generates prompt templates and escalation UI
escalateToL2: production AI orchestration requires Claude Code
realtime AI integration requires Claude Code
AI memory architecture requires Claude Code
cross-service AI orchestration requires Claude Code
escalateToL3: automated dispatch rules require human approval
production AI rollout requires human approval
escalateToL4: factory cannot expose production API keys
factory cannot deploy autonomous agents
factory cannot trigger live operational actions

ai-support-agent-feature.notes: AI support agents are assistants, not autonomous operators. Every AI flow must define an escalation path. Turn storage MUST use Firestore subcollections, not array fields. Polling loops (while status !== completed) are forbidden — use createAndPoll with timeoutSeconds:120.

voice-agent-elevenlabs-feature.description: Realtime voice agent experiences: AI voice onboarding, investor/driver/dispatch voice assistants, embedded landing voice agents, session orchestration.

escalateToL1: factory generates voice UI shell and transcript display
factory generates session state machines and mock adapters
escalateToL2: ElevenLabs production integration requires Claude Code
WebRTC configuration requires Claude Code
audio streaming debugging requires Claude Code
Twilio/SIP integrations require Claude Code
escalateToL3: investor-facing voice statements require human approval
outbound voice automation requires human approval
recording and privacy rules require human approval
escalateToL4: factory cannot expose production voice API keys
factory cannot deploy live realtime WebRTC infrastructure
factory cannot configure production phone routing

voice-agent-elevenlabs-feature.notes: Voice session storage MUST use Firestore subcollection turns, not array fields. Context documents must have status gate (status === approved). Driver voice features must minimize distraction and avoid requiring visual interaction while driving.

landing-react-vite-feature.description: React/Vite web landing projects: investor landings, shipper landings, lead capture pages, AI voice landing experiences, conversion funnels.

escalateToL1: factory generates static sections, lead capture UI, React components
escalateToL2: production deployment requires Claude Code
Firebase Hosting/Vercel/Netlify setup requires Claude Code
GHL webhook integration requires Claude Code
production AI voice integration requires Claude Code
CORS issues require Claude Code
escalateToL3: production campaign launch requires human approval
escalateToL4: factory cannot expose API keys in frontend code
factory cannot modify DNS or hosting settings
factory cannot connect production CRM without approval

landing-react-vite-feature.notes: Landings are part of growth and investor acquisition infrastructure, not simple marketing pages. When landing includes AI voice agent, factory generates UI shell and mock adapter only — production realtime voice requires Claude Code review.
